factors.name: Iran-US Peace Deal (Risk-Off Relief)
factors.summary: US, Iran, and Pakistan report framework deal close to finalization with Hormuz reopening expected. Oil fell below $85 on Friday, reducing input-cost pressures on Dow industrials (CAT, BA, etc.). De-risking of geopolitical tail.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO Success & Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: SpaceX raised $75B in largest IPO ever, shares +19% on debut, Musk became first trillionaire. Massive wealth creation event and validation of growth narrative; retail/institutional risk appetite surging into weekend close.

factors.name: Paramount-WBD Merger DOJ Approval
factors.summary: ~$110B media merger cleared by DOJ on Friday, removing regulatory overhang. Signals permissive antitrust stance under Trump admin, boosts M&A sentiment broadly.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: WTI fell below $85/bbl on Iran deal hopes. Positive for Dow cyclicals (lower energy input costs, transportation costs); negative for CVX/energy but net-neutral to slightly bullish for broad-based industrials.

factors.name: Major US Indices Close Higher Friday
factors.summary: All major indices closed higher on day and week, driven by Iran peace progress and SpaceX momentum. Dow beneficiary of cyclical sentiment lift and reduced geopolitical premium.

factors.name: Macro Data Absent from News Window
factors.summary: No ISM PMI, CPI, NFP, or Fed decisions in past 24h. Upcoming week will feature FOMC, retail sales, and possibly ISM — major volatility risks ahead.

factors.name: Tech-Driven Market Breadth (SpaceX, Anthropic Controls)
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO and AI model export controls (Anthropic) create mixed signals: growth optimism offset by regulatory headwinds on AI. Dow is industrials-heavy, less direct tech exposure than Nasdaq.

factors.name: Dollar & FX Backdrop
factors.summary: DXY traded near 99.80 heading into weekend; no strong USD move reported. Iran deal could weaken USD near-term (less safe-haven demand), modestly positive for multinational Dow earnings translation.

factors.name: Forward Earnings Visibility
factors.summary: No major Dow heavyweight earnings guidance released in this window. CAT, BA, UNH, GS guidance will be crucial for sustained rally; currently opaque.

factors.name: AI Regulation & OpenAI Investigation
factors.summary: Mother sues OpenAI over daughter's death; coalition of state AGs investigating OpenAI; Anthropic forced to disable Fable 5/Mythos 5 per export controls. Rising regulatory/litigation risk on AI, indirectly negative for Dow tech/defense tilt.

screening.narrative: The Dow closed Friday modestly higher (+0.30% 24h) on a cocktail of geopolitical de-risking (Iran peace framework), SpaceX IPO euphoria, and media-merger approval—all de-facto risk-on catalysts. Oil's drop below $85 removes a key cost headwind for industrials. However, the market is now entering a heavy central-bank calendar week (FOMC, BoJ, BoE, RBA decisions), and no major macro data has printed in the past 24h; earnings visibility remains limited. The move is early-stage (only +0.30% 24h) but sentiment has rotated firmly toward cyclicals and away from safe-haven trades.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision & Powell press conference (likely mid-week)
ISM Manufacturing PMI and ISM Services PMI (early week)
Retail Sales & PPI (week of June 16–20)
Durable Goods Orders
China economic data (PMI, industrial production)
Dow heavyweight earnings guidance (CAT, BA, UNH, GS, JPM expected soon)
BoJ, BoE, RBA policy decisions (heavy calendar)
Iran deal signing confirmation (if occurs Mon–Tue)
US Treasury 10-year yield tracking (rate sensitivity for Dow financials & dividend payers)
screening.risk_warning: FOMC could surprise hawkish if Fed signals higher-for-longer rates, flipping risk sentiment sharply. If Iran deal stalls or collapses, oil could spike, crushing Dow cyclicals. Earnings misses from Dow heavyweights (CAT, BA, financials) would invalidate the cyclical thesis. Regulatory crackdown on AI/tech could spillover to Dow growth/defense exposure. SpaceX hype may be a one-day wonder; if it reverses, it could trigger broader risk-off. Major macro data miss (CPI hot, employment weak) would demand rapid repricing of Fed expectations.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is early (+0.30% 24h) but entering Phase 2; wait for a pullback to support (~50,900–51,000) or hold through the FOMC calendar chop. Do not chase the breakout at current levels until FOMC clarity emerges. If Iran deal firms and oil stays sub-$85, resistance near 51,500 becomes viable target by mid-week.

factors.name: Iran ceasefire progress
factors.summary: US-Iran peace deal framework close to signing per Trump admin and Pakistani mediators (Al Jazeera 06:49, 20:17). Removes major geopolitical tail risk to oil, shipping, and risk-on sentiment. Markets closed higher Friday on this news (investingLive 20:25).

factors.name: SpaceX IPO mega-event
factors.summary: Largest IPO in history ($75B raise, $2.2T valuation, +19% first day) boosted sentiment and risk appetite Friday (Bloomberg 22:51, 20:25). Elon Musk became world's first trillionaire, signaling investor confidence in tech/growth narrative. Spillover optimism in broad indices.

factors.name: US equity momentum (24h +0.30%)
factors.summary: Dow up 0.30% over 24h despite flat 4h action suggests intraday consolidation after Friday's rally on Iran/SpaceX drivers. Suggests support being defended.

factors.name: Value-vs-growth rotation ongoing
factors.summary: MarketWatch (22:50) reports value stocks beating growth by wide margin, with earnings broadening beyond tech. For Dow (heavy in industrials, financials, dividend payers), this is constructive but not a fresh trigger.

factors.name: Fed policy / rate expectations
factors.summary: No fresh FOMC data in news window. Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed chairman (Bloomberg 21:11), but no guidance change. Awaiting next week's central bank calendar (FXStreet 20:39) including Fed decision signals.

factors.name: Energy markets stabilizing
factors.summary: Oil near mid-range; Iran deal progress should reduce Strait of Hormuz risk premium. Chevron CEO commentary (Bloomberg 20:51) suggests manageable volatility environment. Benefits Dow energy and transport-heavy composition.

factors.name: China tech headwinds
factors.summary: Pentagon designating Chinese tech firms as 'military companies' (Bloomberg 02:31, Stock Market 02:42) creates export control risk. China 'strongly dissatisfied.' Potential drag on multinational supply chains and Dow members' China revenue (Boeing, CAT, etc.).

factors.name: Treasury yields / rate sensitivity
factors.summary: No fresh 10Y yield data in window. Weekend Swiss population vote and potential EU/Ukraine accession could influence European risk sentiment and trans-Atlantic flows, but indirect for Dow.

factors.name: Retail/consumer sentiment
factors.summary: World Cup optimism in US (USA beat Paraguay 4-1, Los Angeles excitement per Al Jazeera 06:15, 20:00). Symbolic of consumer confidence but no hard retail sales data released.

factors.name: Credit/financial system stress
factors.summary: Korean media conglomerate JoongAng's JTBC broadcaster defaults on securitized loans, downgraded to junk (Bloomberg 05:56). Signals potential liquidity stress in EM/Asia credit. Minor contagion risk but worth monitoring.

screening.narrative: Dow rallied Friday on twin catalysts: Iran ceasefire framework near-finalization (reducing geopolitical risk premium) and SpaceX's record-breaking $75B IPO (boosting risk appetite and confidence in growth/innovation narrative). Post-weekend consolidation now evident (+0.30% 24h, flat 4h) suggesting buyers defending gains rather than extending. Fed policy remains in focus with central bank calendar heavy this week; no fresh inflation/employment data yet. Headwind: China tech export controls could crimp Dow multinational earnings, particularly CAT, BA, and semiconductors.
catalysts_ahead: Fed policy decision and guidance (exact timing TBD, likely this week per FXStreet)
ISM Manufacturing and Services PMI releases (high impact on Dow industrial/cyclical names)
Retail Sales, Durable Goods Orders (consumer/industrial health check)
Dow component earnings (UNH, JPM, GS, CAT, BA forward guidance)
China PMI releases (Caterpillar, Boeing demand signal)
Treasury auction/yield repricing (rate-sensitive Dow financials and dividend payers)
Potential Iran deal finalization announcement (removes geopolitical tail risk if confirmed)
screening.risk_warning: Move already underway post-Iran/SpaceX catalyst Friday; if Iran deal talks stall or collapse, geopolitical risk premium snaps back sharply. China tech export control escalation could trigger EM contagion (JTBC default is canary). Fed tightening signal or inflation surprise would reverse current bullish posture. Valuation stretched after mega-IPO euphoria—pullback to 50,900–51,000 possible before fresh leg higher.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 consolidation; await retest of support near 51,000–51,050 before adding long exposure. If 51,400 resistance reclaimed with volume, consider breakout entry toward 51,600. Avoid chasing at current levels given proximity to resistance and weekend positioning uncertainty.

factors.name: SpaceX IPO / Tech Mega-Cap Rally
factors.summary: SpaceX raised $75B in largest IPO ever (June 12), shares +19% on debut, market cap $2.2T. Musk becomes first trillionaire; spillover confidence for mega-cap tech. However, Dow is only ~15% tech-weighted vs S&P 500, so impact is diluted.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk: Iran-US Deal Talks + Lebanon/Israel Conflict
factors.summary: US-Iran negotiations ongoing ('deal close but Lebanon fighting continues' per Al Jazeera 08:44 UTC), but active Israeli strikes on Lebanon despite ceasefire talks. Oil prices under pressure if deal announced; uncertainty remains high. Dow has energy exposure (CVX) and broad supply-chain risk.

factors.name: Trade Policy / China Headwinds
factors.summary: China 'strongly dissatisfied' with Pentagon labeling of Chinese tech firms (07:48 UTC); US orders Anthropic to disable AI for foreign nationals (09:35 UTC). Escalating tech/export controls could pressure multinationals with China revenue (Boeing, Caterpillar, etc.); Dow has ~50% international exposure.

factors.name: M&A / Corporate Activity
factors.summary: DOJ approves Paramount-WBD $110B merger (22:50 UTC June 12); Roku exploring strategic sale. Corporate deal flow positive; reduces near-term market friction. However, not directly tied to Dow industrials earnings.

factors.name: Earnings Outlook / Dow Heavyweights
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings released in last 24h. Incyte reports positive Phase 3 lymphoma trial (positive for pharma sector, but Incyte not in Dow). Lack of fresh industrial/financial earnings data limits clarity on earnings momentum.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Rate Expectations
factors.summary: ECB's Nagel warns prices stay elevated longer even if Iran war ends (08:23 UTC). No fresh Fed communication or FOMC meeting in immediate window. Rate expectations remain stable; no major hawkish/dovish surprise flagged.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield / Fixed Income
factors.summary: No explicit yield data in news window. ECB inflation language slightly hawkish (prices to stay higher), which could cap yield rallies. Dow financials (GS, JPM, BAC) sensitive to 10Y moves—currently no directional clarity.

factors.name: Dollar Strength / FX
factors.summary: No DXY or forex data in news. Prior context: DXY near 99.80, down 0.27% on week. Iran deal potential could weaken USD if announced (safe-haven bid reduces); Dow multinationals benefit from weaker dollar but headwind from geopolitical risk offsets.

factors.name: Oil & Commodities
factors.summary: No explicit oil price data, but Iran deal talks and US-Iran drone strikes near Strait of Hormuz suggest volatile, risk-off backdrop. CVX (Dow energy exposure) in cautious environment. Tariff/trade friction also caps industrial metals demand (copper, steel).

factors.name: Prior Bias History
factors.summary: Two consecutive BUY6/10 reads (03:33 and 07:45 UTC on June 13) with 'no confirmation triggered'—indicates weak bullish lean but no follow-through. Current 4h move is -0.07%, 24h move is +0.30%; no directional conviction.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught in a tug-of-war between SpaceX IPO euphoria (largest IPO ever, +19% first day, mega-cap tech momentum) and escalating geopolitical friction (Iran-US deal talks stall amid Lebanon fighting, China retaliates on export controls, trade uncertainty). Dow industrials—heavily exposed to Boeing, Caterpillar, and multinational earnings—are cautious on China demand and tariff risk. No major economic data (ISM, CPI, FOMC, earnings) landed in the past 24h, leaving the bias unfocused. Prior two bullish leans found no confirmation, and current price action is flat (±0.1%), indicating consolidation and a lack of follow-through conviction.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision or Fed communication (if scheduled within 48-72h)
ISM Manufacturing PMI & ISM Services PMI release (next scheduled date)
Retail Sales / CPI print (next scheduled)
Dow component earnings (UNH, MSFT, GS, JPM, CAT, BA if imminent)
Iran-US peace deal announcement or breakdown (geopolitical shock potential)
China PMI / growth data (CAT, Boeing, industrials exposure)
Treasury auction / 10Y yield move (affects financials valuation)
screening.risk_warning: SpaceX euphoria could spill into broader equities if retail flows accelerate; China trade escalation or tariff announcement would flip bias sharply BEARISH and test support. Iran deal breakthrough would rally risk assets (equities, crude down, dollar weaker). Conversely, geopolitical escalation or hawkish Fed surprise would undercut Dow. Dow's dividend-heavy, industrial-cyclical nature means rate expectations and global growth outlook dominate—currently muddled.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is in consolidation with no clear directional catalyst in next 24-48h. Avoid chasing; wait for either a break above 51,500 (SpaceX rally confirmation + earnings confidence) or test of 51,000 support (geopolitical/trade risk capitulation) before entering with conviction. Monitor FOMC and Iran deal news for direction.
